description:
To properly set the owner of /var/log/syslog, run the command: 
$ sudo chown syslog /var/log/syslog
rationale:
The <tt>/var/log/syslog</tt> file contains logs of error messages in
the system and should only be accessed by authorized personnel.
ocil_clause: /var/log/syslog does not have an owner of syslog
ocil:
To check the ownership of /var/log/syslog, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /var/log/syslog
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following owner: syslog
fixtext:
To properly set the owner of /var/log/syslog, run the command: 
$ sudo chown syslog /var/log/syslog
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /var/log/syslog file must be owned by syslog.
title: Verify User Who Owns /var/log/syslog File
